suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mors-global-worker skill scope to include native-identity-core and onboarding-handle-profile milestones, since it is actively assigned to features in those milestones.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three rounds of scrutiny reviewers flagged: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:12 lists only relay-foundation/global-async-messaging/realtime-watch/developer-launch-path, but features.json assigns native-identity-core features to mors-global-worker. Three independent reviewers across rounds 1-3 confirmed the same gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance to mors-global-worker skill for stabilization/fix features where the strict 'red test first → green' TDD loop does not apply because failing tests already exist. Workers should be able to mark followedProcedure=true for justified stabilization deviations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker for native-identity-core-fix-deploy-tests-flyctl-env-compat marked followedProcedure=false for a justified stabilization deviation; this pattern will recur for all fix features. Same observation from rounds 1 and 2.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Skill procedure should require manual-check skip rationale when workers mark followedProcedure=true without recording any interactive checks for internal-only primitives.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviewer for native-identity-core-fix-relay-signing-key-fail-closed noted handoff has followedProcedure=true but no interactive checks or skip rationale. Same observation from round 2.

notes: Round 3 re-run after fix feature native-identity-core-fix-signing-key-mismatch-remediation addressed the round 2 blocking issue on native-identity-core-fix-login-relay-key-coordination. All validators pass. The fix reviewer confirmed the original blocking issue (generic mismatch error without remediation) is now resolved with explicit signing-key mismatch handling and regression tests asserting remediation text. One flaky test (cli-encrypted-remote.test.ts 'send --remote --no-encrypt sends plaintext') failed on first full-suite run but passed on re-run and in isolation — likely port collision under concurrent execution.
